fields.narrative_intro: *A scheming person and a bewildered person discuss the Claude Mythos 5 export license underwater.*
fields.panel1:
[setup]
SCHEMING PERSON: "Lutnick personally lifted the export block on Mythos 5."
BEWILDERED PERSON: "The whole government? For one model?"
fields.panel2:
[build]
SCHEMING PERSON: "Only 100-plus 'trusted' institutions get in."
BEWILDERED PERSON: "Trusted by what — a vibe check?"
fields.panel3:
[pivot]
SCHEMING PERSON: "The approved list lives in a physical paper annex."
BEWILDERED PERSON: "Frontier AI gated by a three-ring binder?"
fields.panel4:
[silent reaction]
[Visual: Both float frozen, staring at a laminated 'ANNEX A' clipboard drifting between bubbles. A passing fish reads it.]
fields.panel5:
[punch]
SCHEMING PERSON: "And Claude Fable 5 is still fully blocked."
BEWILDERED PERSON: "You banned the WEAK one?"
fields.panel6:
[button]
[Visual: A velvet rope strung across a coral reef. Bouncer-blob checks Fable 5 against the clipboard.]
BOUNCER: "Mythos, you're in. Fable, not tonight."

narrative_summary: Two underwater blobs marvel that the US government became the bouncer for a frontier-AI nightclub — admitting the powerful Mythos 5 via a paper annex while the weaker Fable 5 stays banned.
rationale: Candidate 4 escalates the absurdity through each panel (a vibe check, a three-ring binder, banning the WEAK one) instead of just restating the bouncer premise, and its final bouncer line lands as an earned button rather than a recap.
story_title: U.S. allows Anthropic to release Mythos AI to 'trusted' US organizations
hat_pair: helmet made entirely of rubber ducks
dunce cap with the word SMART scrawled on it in marker
